steps.title: Identify two independent clauses
steps.content: "With over three millennia of climate data in its tree rings" leads into one IC, and "A single tree like this... can tell the history of the world" is a second IC. The two need a boundary that doesn't create a run-on.

steps.title: Apply the period rule
steps.content:
A period correctly separates two independent clauses. "rings; a" is technically valid syntax, but "a" must be capitalized as "A" since it starts a new sentence — choice B uses lowercase. "and a" creates an awkward join, and "rings, a" is a comma splice.

Only choice A — period followed by capital A — works.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
